message:
fix(state): release read lock before invoking replay handler to prevent callback deadlocks

ReplayEvents previously held s.mu.RLock for the entire duration of the
replay, including the user-provided handler callback. If the handler
tried to call any Store method that acquires a write lock (or even
another read lock from a different goroutine under certain lock
implementations), it would deadlock.

Fix: materialize all event rows into a slice under the read lock, then
release the lock before iterating and calling the handler. This follows
the same "snapshot under lock, process after release" pattern used by
the notification signal emission fix in mcp_agent_mail.

The trade-off is slightly higher memory usage for large replay sets,
but replay is a crash-recovery path that runs once at startup, so the
allocation is negligible compared to the deadlock risk.

Tests (state_test.go):
- 54 lines of new tests covering replay handler isolation, including
  a test that verifies the handler can call back into the Store without
  deadlocking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
